1b_1a__summary: CoC partners with Black and Brown-led nonprofits, includes PLE from those communities on its Board and committees, and uses race-disaggregated CE data via a dashboard to monitor for equity.

CoC operates the Andre Ellison Equity Fund supporting BIPOC-led CBOs, embeds equity scoring in local competition with input from people with lived experience, and analyzes race/ethnicity data through CE Redesign.

CoC partners with a Black-led church for re-entry job fairs and with the State Attorney's Office for expungement events, framed as addressing over-incarceration of Black and African American households.

NYC CoC expanded its provider community to include multiple POC-led organizations serving diverse underserved populations, and formed the Racial Equity in Supportive Housing (RESH) Workgroup to advance racial and wage equity for Black supportive-housing stakeholders.

CoC runs Racial Equity Impact Assessments on program design and procurement, engages the Lived Experience Coalition and Youth Action Board in program design, and examines performance outcomes by race through a dedicated Racial Equity Workgroup.
